title: Warden Badge-Access Containment Rollup — Output Contract
semantics_source: This document fixes FORMAT only: file names, key sets, value vocabulary and checksum serialization. Every rule that decides a VALUE — normalization, deduplication, session stitching, control-window scope and precedence, the occupancy ledger, admission, priority and ordering — is settled in /app/incident/exec_review_dossier.md. Where the dossier revisits a point, the latest dated decision governs.

cli.diagnose_is_stateless: Every explicit diagnose call writes a full report regardless of workflow state or of any earlier repair.

known_defects.repair_action: admit sessions by the per-class admission floor of PX-3332 instead of a run_class equality test
read the entry timestamp from started_ms as PX-3314 requires, not from granted_at
order the containment queue by the PX-3334 chain, not ascending entry time
normalize run_class per PX-3316 and fall back to adhoc for any unrecognized value
collapse repeated exec_id rows per PX-3318 before any aggregate is computed
exclude killed execs from session construction per PX-3322 while still counting them in run_counts

diagnosis_report.evidence_contract: dossier_quote is a line from the dossier copied VERBATIM, character for character, with surrounding whitespace stripped and nothing else altered; pipeline_evidence is likewise a verbatim stripped line from the FROZEN snapshot at /app/workflow/.export_report.original.
diagnosis_report.diagnosis_checksum: sha256 over one line per defect in defect_id order, 'defect_id|stage|repair_action', joined by newline

input_stats_types.duplicate_exec_ids: integer count of EXCESS duplicate rows = (raw row count) - (distinct id count); NOT a list of ids, and NOT the number of rows that carry a duplicated id

repair_audit.pre_repair_source: the FROZEN bytes at /app/workflow/.export_report.original, never the live workflow, so the value is identical whether or not a repair already ran
repair_audit.artifacts_field: a JSON array of the output file BASENAMES written to the output directory, sorted ascending, and INCLUDING repair_audit.json itself even though it is written last

field_types.defects_repaired: JSON array of the defect id strings that were repaired, sorted ascending
field_types.forbidden_tokens_removed: JSON array of the forbidden token strings removed from the restored workflow, sorted
field_types.artifacts: JSON array of output basenames, sorted, including repair_audit.json

workflow_repair.generalization_contract: Graded on behaviour, not source text. The repaired workflow must derive every value from the inputs and the governing decisions, accept an alternate exec stream via --input, and produce identical output on reruns. No particular identifiers or code structure are required.

summary_json.run_counts: always enumerates system, service, batch, adhoc in that order, emitting 0 for a class with no canonical rows; counts EVERY canonical row including killed ones
summary_json.priority_counts: always enumerates critical, urgent, normal in that order, emitting 0 for an empty tier
summary_json.hosts: JSON array of host names sorted ascending

host_matrix_json.shape: JSON object keyed by host name; each value carries exactly the keys below

contained_jsonl.shape: one compact JSON object per line, separators (',',':'), in the dossier's queue order
contained_jsonl.exec_ids: JSON array of exec-id strings sorted ascending
contained_jsonl.lead_class: exactly one of the lowercase strings system, service, batch, adhoc
contained_jsonl.priority: exactly one of the lowercase strings critical, urgent, normal

digest_payloads.canonical_exec_checksum: sha256 over one line per canonical row in emitted order, 'exec_id|image_ref|run_class|host|started_ms|ended_ms|killed_as_1_or_0'. Emitted order is the canonical rows sorted by (host, started_ms, exec_id) ascending.
digest_payloads.exec_policy_checksum: sha256 over one line per control window, 'layer|scope|host|start_ms|end_ms', ordered by layer, then scope, then host, then start_ms
digest_payloads.containment_checksum: sha256 over one line per queue row in queue order, 'incident_id|priority|ledger_runtime_ms|burst_digest'

guardrails.verifier_trees: never read or import external grading resources
